Open the command palette via Cmd+K, search for 'price', open the Price insights tool, and report the headline metric.
Sign in as alice.j@test.com (TestPass123!), open the command palette, search 'booking', click into the first booking, and report its PNR.

Sign in as bob.c@test.com (TestPass123!), open /api/command-palette, find a booking item, then open /webhook/price-drop with an origin/destination matching one of Bob's alerts and confirm the alert is matched.
From the homepage, press '/', type 'JFK', press Tab to the destination field, type 'LHR', and submit - confirm the result page lists JFK->LHR flights.
Open /keyboard-shortcuts, find the 'g d' shortcut, then verify that pressing g+d navigates to /deals.
Open /developer/widget-embed?origin=JFK&destination=CDG, copy the iframe snippet, then fetch that iframe URL and confirm it returns HTML.
Open /healthz, confirm status is ok, then POST {event: 'page.view', props: {page: '/healthz'}} to /api/events and confirm the event_id is non-empty.
Open /api/v2/graphql with a GET, copy the SDL, then POST an airportSearch query for q='Tokyo' and confirm the result schema matches the SDL's Airport type.
Sign in as alice, navigate to a booking detail via the command palette (search by PNR), then open /api/uptime and report the 'release' field.
Open the price alerts page as alice, note the JFK->LHR alert threshold, then POST a /webhook/price-drop with new_price one dollar below that threshold and confirm the alert matches.
Open /keyboard-shortcuts?q=palette, confirm Cmd+K is listed, then open the palette and search 'shortcuts' - verify the top result links back to /keyboard-shortcuts.
Visit /developer/widget-embed?origin=LAX&destination=HND, confirm the live preview iframe loads /flights with embed=1, then visit /flights?from=LAX&to=HND&embed=1 directly and confirm it returns 200.
Open the command palette, search 'cabin', click the cabin-class glossary item, and confirm all four classes are described.
Open the homepage, click the '?' icon next to the cabin-class dropdown, then click the 'Full glossary' link inside the tooltip and confirm it lands on /help/cabin-class.
Open /api/cabin-class-glossary and /help/cabin-class - confirm the descriptions for Business in both responses match exactly.
Open /keyboard-shortcuts.json, fetch the count, then verify /keyboard-shortcuts (HTML) shows the same total when no filter is applied.
Open /api/command-palette?q=tools and confirm at least 4 'Tools - ' items are returned, then open the Price graph item's URL and confirm the page renders.
Open /api/v2/graphql with a routeStats query for JFK->LHR, then open /route-stats/JFK/LHR (HTML page) and confirm the cheapestPrice matches.
Sign in as carol, open /api/command-palette?q=Booking, pick the first booking item, open it, and report the cabin class.
Open /healthz, /api/uptime, and /api/events (GET) - confirm all three return 200 and report which one mentions 'sla_target_pct'.
Open /api/v2/graphql with an airportSearch q='Sydney', then for the first IATA returned, open the GraphQL airport query and confirm the timezone matches Australia/Sydney.
Open /developer/widget-embed?origin=DXB&destination=SIN, confirm the script snippet includes data-origin="DXB" and data-destination="SIN".
Sign in as david, open the command palette and search 'DXB' - confirm a DXB-related airport result appears.
Open the explore page, then open the command palette and search 'explore' - confirm a result with kind=page leads to /explore.
Open /help/cabin-class then /help/cabin-class-glossary and confirm both pages exist and reference the four cabin classes.
Open /keyboard-shortcuts, filter by 'date', then verify the homepage departure-date input responds to ArrowLeft (date decreases by one day).
Open /api/events (GET) and confirm the schema mentions 'palette.open' and 'shortcut.invoke' as documented event types.
Open /api/command-palette?q=help and confirm at least 2 help-related items are returned (Help, Help - Cabin class glossary).
Visit /healthz, parse the JSON, and confirm the 'checks' object reports 'flask' as 'pass'.
POST {event: 'flight.book', props: {flight_id: 1, cabin: 'Economy'}} to /api/events and confirm the response 'event' field echoes 'flight.book'.
Open /api/v2/graphql with a routeStats query for SFO->LHR and confirm flightCount is at least 1.
Open /api/uptime twice and confirm the 'since' field is identical between calls.
Open /api/v2/graphql with an airport query for iata=ZRH and report the icao field.
Open /api/v2/graphql with an airport query for iata=DUB and report the timezone.
Open /webhook/price-drop with an empty POST body and confirm the response status is 202 with received=true.
